title: Abbreviations — Expand or Keep for the Reader
brief: For each abbreviation, decide whether you should KEEP it (the reader will recognise it) or EXPAND it (the reader is non-specialist or lay).

items.rationale: PRN is universally understood by pharmacists.

items.context: Writing a letter directly to the patient
items.rationale: Patient may not recognise the abbreviation.

items.context: Writing to a school nurse with no medical training
items.rationale: School nurse role is lay; expand for clarity.
MI is standard between medical professionals.

items.context: Writing to a non-medical occupational therapist
items.rationale: Latin dose abbreviations should be expanded for non-medical readers.

items.context: Writing to a hospital social worker
items.rationale: Casual clinical shorthand — never appropriate in formal OET letters.
